text: If the opponent has an open four, blocking is usually mandatory.
Control near the center usually gives more extension options in Gomoku.
Building connected threats can force the opponent into passive defense.
A common mistake is expanding attack while ignoring an immediate defensive response.
When a forced winning line appears, prioritizing it is often stronger than positional expansion.
Players who prioritize blocking and reducing risk often show a stable and defense-oriented playstyle.
Players who consistently create threats and push initiative often have an aggressive attacking personality.
Players who shift between offense and defense according to the board tend to show a balanced and rational style.
Ignoring direct threats for speculative attacks may indicate an impulsive or risk-seeking playstyle.
Players who rely on punishing mistakes and seizing sudden chances often show an opportunistic personality.
A player whose later moves become more stable after early mistakes may show a growth-oriented personality.
